personas.name: Budget-Conscious Mental Health Seeker
personas.description: A user who downloaded MindDoc hoping to access meaningful mental health support for free, but feels misled when nearly all useful features are locked behind a subscription they cannot afford.
goals: Access mental health tools and resources without paying a subscription fee
Understand what the app offers before committing to a purchase
Use mood tracking and insight features to manage mental wellbeing at no cost
pain_points: Almost all features are behind a paywall after a short free period
The 14-day free assessment ends with a paywall rather than actionable insights
Feels deceived after investing time answering questions only to be asked to pay
Subscription pricing feels expensive relative to perceived free value
context: Cannot afford or is unwilling to pay for a mental health app subscription
May be in a financially vulnerable position where mental health support is most needed
Compares the app to free alternatives that offer more without payment
constraints: Will not subscribe unless free features are genuinely useful
Expects transparency about what is free vs. paid before starting the onboarding

personas.name: Daily Mood Tracker
personas.description: A user who relies on MindDoc's daily check-in questions to monitor their emotional wellbeing over time. They value the routine of multiple daily check-ins and appreciate the patterns and insights that emerge over weeks of consistent tracking.
goals: Log mood and emotions three times a day to detect patterns
Review weekly and monthly summaries to understand emotional trends
Use mood history to communicate mental state to a therapist
pain_points: Limited emotion vocabulary in the check-in options
Cannot easily export or share mood data with healthcare providers
Free version restricts access to detailed reports and long-term history
context: Uses the app daily as part of a mental health management routine
May also be working with a therapist and uses the app as a supplementary tool
Values quantified self-knowledge about emotional wellbeing
constraints: Needs check-in flow to be quick and frictionless
Expects mood history and reports to be accessible without heavy subscription requirements

personas.name: Habit-Building Reminder Dependent
personas.description: A user who struggles to remember to check in with their mental health without prompts. They depend on push notifications from MindDoc to maintain their daily logging habit, but experience inconsistent or poorly timed notifications that disrupt their routine.
goals: Receive reliable push notifications at consistent, user-set times throughout the day
Maintain a daily check-in habit without having to remember manually
Customize the number and timing of daily reminders
pain_points: Notifications sometimes stop working without explanation
Notification timing is inflexible or arrives at inconvenient times
Easy to miss notifications leading to gaps in tracking data
Too many or too few notifications without fine-grained control
context: Has a busy schedule and relies on reminders to build and sustain habits
May deal with depression or low motivation, making self-prompted check-ins unreliable
Uses the notification as a trigger for a mindful pause during the day
constraints: Notification delivery must be reliable across Android versions and battery optimization modes
Must be able to schedule notifications at specific custom times

personas.name: Privacy-Aware Mental Health User
personas.description: A user who is cautious about sharing sensitive mental health data with a mobile app. They are concerned about third-party data sharing, mandatory account creation, and lack of security features that would protect their personal health information.
goals: Use the app without sharing health data with third parties
Option to use the app anonymously or with minimal personal information
Protect mood and journal data with a PIN or biometric lock
Control what data is shared and with whom
pain_points: App requires mandatory account creation before any use
Terms of service require agreeing to data sharing with third parties
No PIN or biometric lock to protect sensitive in-app data
App does not function offline, raising concerns about data transmission
context: Treats mental health data as highly sensitive personal information
Aware of data privacy regulations and expects GDPR-compliant practices
May stop using the app if privacy concerns are not addressed
constraints: Will not use the app if mandatory data sharing cannot be disabled
Requires clear, plain-language privacy policy before creating an account

personas.name: Structured Mental Health Learner
personas.description: A user who engages deeply with MindDoc's therapeutic exercises, educational content, and structured mindfulness programs. They appreciate the clinical foundation of the content and use it alongside or in preparation for professional therapy.
goals: Complete CBT-based and mindfulness exercises to develop coping strategies
Learn about mental health conditions through in-app educational content
Use structured programs (e.g., stress management, mindfulness) to improve wellbeing
Prepare topics or data to discuss with a therapist
pain_points: Most structured programs and exercises are locked behind the premium subscription
Content does not adapt dynamically to the user's current mental health state
Program completion tracking could be more visible
context: May have been recommended the app by a doctor, therapist, or mental health professional
Engages with the app as part of a broader self-improvement or mental health recovery journey
Values evidence-based, professional content over generic wellness tips
constraints: Needs access to at least a baseline set of exercises in the free tier
Exercises and programs must be clearly explained and clinically grounded
